subject: Bug Fix- Updated the http response code/ response message
commit_message:
Bug Fix- Updated the http response code/ response message

Issue-ID: CCSDK-2828
Change-Id: Ifa65c7a75fdc00cb0db5fd34e3a42695bad3c4db
Signed-off-by: Lathish <lathishbabu.ganesan@est.tech>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/ccsdk-distribution-maven-verify-master-mvn36-openjdk11/270/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/ccsdk-distribution-maven-verify-master-mvn36-openjdk11/270

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/ccsdk-distribution-review-verification-maven-master/178/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/ccsdk-distribution-review-verification-maven-master/178

Patch Set 1:

I am not sure if I misunderstand something.
Where is the change for putA1Policy? Isn't it the one that is returning wrong response code, i.e., returning 200 (instead of 201) when a new A1 policy is created?
Other rest calls are already returning correct response codes I guess.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> I am not sure if I misunderstand something.
> Where is the change for putA1Policy? Isn't it the one that is returning wrong response code, i.e., returning 200 (instead of 201) when a new A1 policy is created?
> Other rest calls are already returning correct response codes I guess.

Thanks for pointing it. Added now.
Actually the bug was irrespective of the actual response code/message sent by the ric the DG's hard coded the response code/message for success/failure scenario. 
Now it is sending the actual response code/message it is received from the RIC.

> > Other rest calls are already returning correct response codes I guess.
> 
> Thanks for pointing it. Added now.
> Actually the bug was irrespective of the actual response code/message sent by the ric the DG's hard coded the response code/message for success/failure scenario. 
> Now it is sending the actual response code/message it is received from the RIC.
Change has been successfully merged by Dan Timoney
